scenario_a.label: Frozen Russian Assets
scenario_a.description: G7-frozen Russian sovereign and central-bank assets held in Euroclear and other custodians

scenario_a.label: G7 ERA Loan Proceeds
scenario_a.description: Extraordinary Revenue Acceleration loan backed by frozen-asset interest proceeds

scenario_a.label: EU Ukraine Facility
scenario_a.description: EU Ukraine Facility grants and loans (EUR 50 bn ≈ USD 54 bn at 2024 rate)

scenario_a.label: Bilateral Donors
scenario_a.description: Pledged bilateral donor contributions (US, UK, Japan, Canada, etc.)
scenario_a.source_label: RDNA3 Donor Pledge Registry, World Bank 2024

scenario_a.label: Diaspora Bonds
scenario_a.description: Ukrainian diaspora sovereign bonds (Israel Bonds precedent: $44 bn over 70 years)
scenario_a.source_label: Israel Bonds precedent — diaspora sovereign debt market

scenario_a.label: Privatisation Proceeds
scenario_a.description: Receipts from post-war privatisation of state-owned enterprises

scenario_b.label: Russian Reparations (Direct)
scenario_b.description: Direct reparations under a post-war legal settlement — KSE claim is $486 bn
scenario_b.source_label: KSE Institute 'Russia Will Pay' — $486 bn claim, 2024

scenario_b.label: Frozen Asset Transfer
scenario_b.description: Full transfer of frozen Russian sovereign assets under UN or G7 legal framework

scenario_b.label: EU Successor Programme
scenario_b.description: Post-2027 EU reconstruction programme succeeding the Ukraine Facility

scenario_b.label: Bilateral Donors
scenario_b.description: Bilateral donor contributions in a post-war environment (likely higher confidence)
scenario_b.source_label: RDNA3 Donor Pledge Registry, World Bank 2024

scenario_b.label: Diaspora Bonds
scenario_b.description: Ukrainian diaspora sovereign bonds — higher confidence post-war
scenario_b.source_label: Israel Bonds precedent — diaspora sovereign debt market

scenario_b.label: Privatisation Proceeds
scenario_b.description: Post-war privatisation of state-owned enterprises and recaptured assets
